title: Senate President pro Tempore Mike McGuire (D-North Coast) and Senate Budget and Fiscal Review Committee Chair Senator Scott Wiener (D-San Francisco) released the following statemen

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The increasing state deficit is contributed to by the cost of the January wildfires in Los Angeles, federal cuts to medical care, and economic upheaval caused by federal tariffs.",
    "Senator Dave Cortese opposes proposed federal cuts to Medicaid and a state proposal to scale back healthcare commitments for undocumented residents.",
    "Proposed cuts to Medi-Cal could affect the health care of over 72,000 patients at Santa Clara Valley Medical Center.",
    "More than 14 million Californians depend on Medi-Cal, including half of all patients in Santa Clara County's public hospitals.",
    "Federal budget cuts pose a threat to public health, equity, and fiscal stability.",
    "The Governor's May Revision preserves California's core investments in transitional kindergarten and universal meals.",
    "Senator Dave Cortese's bill SB 743 aims to create a more equitable funding formula for education without detracting from existing school budgets.",
    "The California High-Speed Rail project has created 14,600 high-quality jobs and engaged 880 small businesses.",
    "The High-Speed Rail project will prevent 142 million metric tons of carbon dioxide emissions.",
    "The Governor's plan to distribute $3.3 billion in grant funding from Proposition 1 will create more than 5,000 residential treatment beds and more than 21,800 outpatient treatment slots for behavioral health care services in California.",
    "The homeless population in need of mental health services continues to grow, necessitating faster and smarter building of residential units."
  ]
}
```
